title: Duplex-mode remediation for self interference
abstract: Systems and methods in which one or more wireless communication stations implement duplex-mode remediation to facilitate the signal communication when an issue with respect to self-interference is identified are described. Duplex-mode remediation may be implemented for various communication scenarios, such as for random access phase communications, for communications having different priorities, etc. A wireless communication station may implement one or more changes with respect to the full duplex operation of the wireless communication station based upon determining that an issue with respect to self-interference may be, or is being, experienced, such as to provide for a wireless communication station operating in an in-band full duplex mode to increase interference cancellation and/or to fall back to a sub-band full duplex mode or a half duplex mode, to provide for a wireless communication station operating in a sub-band full duplex mode to increase interference cancellation and/or to fall back to a half duplex mode.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of wireless communication, comprising: analyzing, by a wireless communication station operating in a full duplex mode, one or more aspects of signal communication with regard to self-interference; and implementing, by the wireless communication station, duplex-mode remediation by falling back from the full duplex mode to facilitate the signal communication when the analyzing determines an aspect of the one or more aspects of the signal communication presents an issue with respect to self-interference. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the falling back from the full duplex mode comprises full duplex fall back selected from a group comprising: falling back to a sub-band full duplex mode from an in-band full duplex mode; falling back to a half duplex mode from the in-band full duplex mode; and falling back to the half duplex mode from the sub-band full duplex mode. 3. The method of claim 1, further comprising: transmitting, by the wireless communication station in the full duplex mode, a first message of a random access procedure; and monitoring, by the wireless communication station, for a second message of the random access procedure responsive to the first message, wherein the aspect of the signal communication determined to present an issue with respect to self-interference comprises identifying a failure to receive the second message. 4. The method of claim 1, further comprising: transmitting, by the wireless communication station in a sub-band full duplex mode, a first message of a random access procedure; and monitoring, by the wireless communication station, for a second message of the random access procedure, wherein the implementing the duplex-mode remediation by falling back from the full duplex mode alters the sub-band full duplex mode when the analyzing determines that the second message has not been received after transmission of the first message a threshold number of times. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein altering the sub-band full duplex mode by the implementing duplex -mode remediation comprises: falling back to a half duplex mode from the sub-band full duplex mode. 6. The method of claim 6, further comprising: monitoring, by the wireless communication station operating in the full duplex mode, for a first message of a random access procedure, wherein the aspect of the signal communication determined to present an issue with respect to self-interference comprises identifying a failure to receive the first message. 7. The method of claim 8, wherein the implementing duplex-mode remediation by falling back from the full duplex mode comprises: falling back to a half duplex mode from the full duplex mode. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the analyzing one or more aspects of signal communication determines that the signal communication comprises transmission or reception of a first communication and reception or transmission of a second communication, wherein the first communication is of a higher priority than the second communication, and wherein the implementing the duplex-mode remediation by falling back from the full duplex mode alters the full duplex mode to facilitate communication of the first communication when the analyzing determines that an issue with respect to self-interference is present. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the full duplex mode comprises an in-band full duplex mode, and wherein altering the full duplex mode comprises: falling back to a sub-band full duplex mode from the in-band full duplex mode. 10. The method of claim 8, wherein the full duplex mode comprises an in-band full duplex mode, and wherein altering the full duplex mode comprises: falling back to a half duplex mode from the in-band full duplex mode. 11. The method of claim 8, wherein the full duplex mode comprises a sub-band full duplex mode, and wherein altering the full duplex mode comprises: falling back to a half duplex mode from the sub-band full duplex mode. 12. An apparatus configured for wireless communication, comprising: means for analyzing, by a wireless communication station operating in a full duplex mode, one or more aspects of signal communication with regard to self-interference; and 

 means for implementing, by the wireless communication station, duplex-mode remediation by falling back from the full duplex mode to facilitate the signal communication when the analyzing determines an aspect of the one or more aspects of the signal communication presents an issue with respect to self-interference. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, further comprising: means for transmitting, by the wireless communication station in the full duplex mode, a first message of a random access procedure; and means for monitoring, by the wireless communication station, for a second message of the random access procedure responsive to the first message, wherein the aspect of the signal communication determined to present an issue with respect to self-interference comprises identifying a failure to receive the second message. 14. The apparatus of claim 12, further comprising: means for transmitting, by the wireless communication station in the sub-band full duplex mode, a first message of a random access procedure; and means for monitoring, by the wireless communication station, for a second message, wherein duplex-mode remediation by falling back from the full duplex mode alters the sub band full duplex mode by falling back to a half duplex mode from the sub-band full duplex mode when the means for analyzing determines that the second message has not been received after transmission of the first message a threshold number of times. 15. The apparatus of claim 12, further comprising: means for monitoring, by the wireless communication station operating in the full duplex mode, for a first message of a random access procedure, wherein the aspect of the signal communication determined to present an issue with respect to self-interference comprises identifying a failure to receive the first message. 16. A non-transitory computer-readable medium having program code configured for wireless communication recorded thereon, the program code executable by one or more processors for causing the one or more processors to: analyze, by a wireless communication station operating in a full duplex mode, one or more aspects of signal communication with regard to self-interference; and implement, by the wireless communication station, duplex-mode remediation by falling back from the full duplex mode to facilitate the signal communication when 

analyzing determines an aspect of the one or more aspects of the signal communication presents an issue with respect to self-interference. 17. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 16, wherein the program code further causes the one or more processors to: transmit, by the wireless communication station in the full duplex mode, a first message of a random access procedure; and monitor, by the wireless communication station, for a second message of the random access procedure responsive to the first message, wherein the aspect of the signal communication determined to present an issue with respect to self-interference comprises identifying a failure to receive the second message. 18. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 16, wherein the program code further causes the one or more processors to: transmit, by the wireless communication station in a sub-band full duplex mode, a first message of a random access procedure; and monitor, by the wireless communication station, for a second message of the random access procedure, wherein the duplex -mode remediation by falling back from the full duplex mode alters the sub-band full duplex mode by falling back to a half duplex mode from the sub-band full duplex mode when analyzing determines that the second message has not been received after transmission of the first message a threshold number of times. 19. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 16, wherein the program code further causes the one or more processors to: monitor, by the wireless communication station operating in the full duplex mode, for a first message of the random access procedure, wherein the aspect of the signal communication determined to present an issue with respect to self-interference comprises identifying a failure to receive the first message. 20. An apparatus configured for wireless communication, the apparatus comprising: at least one processor; and at least one memory communicatively coupled with the at least one processor and storing processor-readable code that, when executed by the at least one processor is configured to: 

 analyze, by a wireless communication station operating in a full duplex mode, one or more aspects of signal communication with regard to self-interference; and implement, by the wireless communication station, duplex-mode remediation by falling back from the full duplex mode to facilitate the signal communication when the analyzing determines an aspect of the one or more aspects of the signal communication presents an issue with respect to self-interference. 21. The apparatus of claim 20, wherein the falling back from the full duplex mode comprises full duplex fall back selected from the group consisting of: falling back to a sub-band full duplex mode from an in-band full duplex mode; falling back to a half duplex mode from the in-band full duplex mode; and falling back to a half duplex mode from the sub-band full duplex mode. 22. The apparatus of claim 20, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: transmit, by the wireless communication station in the full duplex mode, a first message of a random access procedure; and monitor, by the wireless communication station, for a second message of the random access procedure responsive to the first message, wherein the aspect of the signal communication determined to present an issue with respect to self-interference comprises identifying a failure to receive the second message. 23. The apparatus of claim 20, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: transmit, by the wireless communication station in a sub-band full duplex mode, a first message of a random access procedure; and monitor, by the wireless communication station, for a second message of the random access procedure, wherein implementing the duplex-mode remediation by falling back from the duplex mode alters the sub-band full duplex mode when analyzing determines that the second message has not been received after transmission of the first message a threshold number of times. 24. The apparatus of claim 23, wherein the at least one processor is configured to: alter the sub-band full duplex mode by falling back to a half duplex mode from the sub-band full duplex mode. 25. The apparatus of claim 20, wherein the at least one processor is further to: monitor, by the wireless communication station operating in the full duplex mode, for a first message of a random access procedure, wherein the aspect of the signal communication determined to present an issue with respect to self-interference comprises identifying a failure to receive the first message. 26. The apparatus of claim 25, wherein the at least one processor is configured to: implement the duplex-mode remediation by falling back to a half duplex mode from the full duplex mode. 27. The apparatus of claim 20, wherein the at least one processor configured to analyze one or more aspects of signal communication determines that the signal communication comprises the transmission or reception of a first communication and the reception or transmission of a second communication, wherein the first communication is of a higher priority than the second communication, and implement the duplex-mode remediation by falling back from the full duplex mode to alter the full duplex mode to facilitate communication of the first communication when the analyzing determines that an issue with respect to self-interference is present. 28. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein the full duplex mode comprises an in-band full duplex mode, and wherein the at least one processor is configured to: alter the full duplex mode by falling back to a sub-band full duplex mode from the in- band full duplex mode. 29. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein the full duplex mode comprises an in-band full duplex mode, and wherein the at least one processor is configured to: alter the full duplex mode by falling back to a half duplex mode from the in-band full duplex mode. 30. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein the full duplex mode comprises a sub band full duplex mode, and wherein the at least one processor is configured to: alter the full duplex mode by falling back to a half duplex mode from the sub-band full duplex mode.
